text.title: Improving i and c — Viral Optimization Levers
text.summary: Tactics to increase invites-per-user (i) and invite conversion rate (c), with common mistakes to avoid.

list.item: Natural share moments: prompt sharing at the instant of an achievement or result — not on first login
Referral incentives: reward referrers; double-sided (both parties rewarded) converts better than single-sided
Multi-channel sharing: email, SMS, copy-link, social — users have channel preferences
Contact import: allow bulk invite from address book to lower per-invite friction
Viral content: shareable artifacts (results page, badge, score) that embed product branding
Team/collaboration features: product that requires others to use inherently drives invites
Personal invitation copy: "John invited you to [Product]" vs generic blast — high impact
Social proof: "5 of your colleagues already use this" — high impact
Clear value proposition on landing page: one sentence, no jargon
Low-friction signup: one-click social auth, minimal fields — high impact
Incentive for invitee: "$10 when you join" — medium impact, verify margin allows it
Preview of value before signup: show what they will get, not just marketing copy
Track: invite_sent event with loop_type and share_channel properties
Compute i, c, K weekly; flag K drops > 20% week-over-week
Segment K by acquisition channel and user cohort to find viral power users
Do not report K computed on fewer than 500 invites — noise dominates at small samples

antipattern.description: Over-incentivizing referrals (50%+ reward to hit K=1).
antipattern.reason: Attracts fraud and low-quality users who joined for the reward. Sets unsustainable cost expectations. K plateaus quickly; agents optimizing reward size erode margin without meaningful K lift.

antipattern.description: Using a single invite channel (email only).
antipattern.reason: Users have strong channel preferences. Email-only sharing misses SMS and social invites that convert at 25% vs email's 12-15% in many consumer apps.

antipattern.description: Sending generic invite blasts from the product (outbreak/spam virality).
antipattern.reason: Modern email filters and OS protections block automated contact imports. Damages brand NPS and can trigger CAN-SPAM violations.
